item.title: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded:
Posting here for clarity.

Noting that as the original content creator uploaded the image onto the wiki, they are automatically releasing the image under Creative Commons BY-SA 3.0. If the content creator did not intend for this to happen, please contact Licensing Staff.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm 1800 stories in and this is my first comment. I love
it to bits!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Growing up sometimes sucks for those who are different, and children are capable of incredible cruelty. Inflicting trauma for the sake of it, and for no better reason than "this person exists, and I don't like that."

This SCP resonated with me harder than I'd like. As someone who was treated similarly (sans the physical cutting), and retreated into books, I almost couldn't finish this.

So take my +1, my feels, and I'm going to go read something significantly happier now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hello Hannah, thank you very much for that. I read the story. Well I do not plan for him to wake up, actually, but I like the vivid depiction of his asocial reaction to the researchers.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I know…….All the SCP story is not true.

But It was the first time that I wanted an SCP to wake up……

Also I wrote a story in Chinese about SCP-1830-A.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My sincere apologies for downvoting this earlier; this just touches me on a level too personal for me. I won't go into it; all I can say is that I share too much in common with SCP-1830-A.

It's a really good story. I want to upvote it for the story and story-telling, but I can't. At the same time, I know I shouldn't have downvoted a good story either.

I'm leaving this at neutral. I may never be able to upvote it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Why would it matter if it was, if it was a competently written SCP?

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The setting is actually Mei Lam public estate in Tai Wai, Shatin, where I spent my childhood. I studied in one of the secondary schools there, which shared building with another school (or I should say it was "merged" with that other school, to be precise). Although my school weren't ranked too high in term of conducts, that neighboring school was downright gangland, to the point where it was ranked band 5 back when there was a band 5 ranking. (I'm a post-80s) There was no bullying as serious as that, yep, but there was where I got the inspiration from.

Next to my school there was a primary school, and next to it was a small soccer yard where 1830 was plighted. Yea, all of it are real locations, it's just that many of the schools are renamed today.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As a Hongkonger, I am proud of this and as a guy interested in military history, this was really well done. Nice to know that the KMT/GMD get acknowledged too.

Also, hopefully the kid's gonna get revenge some day. We didn't have bullying like that so far, but it's still very fucked up.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: awww. sad +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As a girl who was horribly bullied all throughout elementary and high school, and who turned to art as an escape…this really, really, really resonated with me… +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I just…

Sorry, but this very deeply upsets me on an extremely personal level.

Please excuse me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
But Mr. Mok, I didn't WANNA go on a feels trip ;'(
God, poor guy. Not even reality bending can protect him from those assholes. +1 for you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wonderfully sad, but personally I feel the interviews add nothing. I'd already pieced together all the information presented therein, and didn't get much extra emotion. Drawing is great, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
He finally cried.

Me too.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Definition 4a

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh gosh really?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Damnit, these feels.

+1

However, there is one thing that bothers me.

Examination found extensive contusions on the head, arms and trunk.

A trunk? That makes me think that he's an elephant or something. Is this an error?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks. This is actually a direction I love: supernatural power can't save one from being overwhelmed by the reality. The power is not a blessing, but a curse. I was really inspired by SCP-1723 in taking this direction

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This makes me really sad, especially the bit at the end. He tries to save himself with his powers… but it doesn't even work. Even with SCP powers, the poor guy can't get a break.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's not. At least not the interview bit. But I did draw inspirations from my school experience and what I heard from the social service center I worked in.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I hope it's not a self-insert

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Touching enough for a +1
I'd say the suggestion that they're doing something to the kids genitals is kinda unnecessary and personally I find it detracts from the article, I like it overall though. Nice drawing too.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is something else. Very emotional, and very well-done. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Take it! Take it all away! My feels ; A ;

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Please forgive my saying this, but…

I'm so glad you can believe that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was bullied as a child. They always got away with it and the teachers blamed me for crying and not ignoring them hard enough. This article was stomach-churning and painful.

+1

(P.S. I think "is marked in red print to indicate" ought to be something like "is marked with a red line to indicate")

item.title: Re: Small language issues and suggested corrections
item.content:encoded: Thanks for accepting many of those corrections. Looks a lot better now. :-)

item.title: Re: Small language issues and suggested corrections
item.content:encoded:
Thanks for the checking! As the author I'd say that such an amount of errors deserves a self-downvote… No, it's just a joke.

Incised wounds, curved into derogatory phrases in traditional Chinese characters, are found on the pelvis and inner thighs.

That's indeed a major mistake.

The German Army in World War One (3): 1917-1918, Osprey Publishing. Extensive handwritten translations were found in "Weapon", "Training" and illustration plates' commentary sections.

I have just checked the book, and found that I have mistaken its context with another Osprey book. Therefore I have replace "Weapon", "Training" into "Strategy & Tactics", which genuinely exist on that book

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That's the reply I'm waiting for!

One of the messages I intend to convey in this story is: the lack of justice for bullying. After all those year of being treated like toy, being threatened, being beaten, being humiliated, and finding your mind reliving those miserable years whenever you are alone, you find those people who break you walking free and enjoying their adult life. There's no retribution for them, only eternal suffering for you, because you are different. This point is emphasized by that after all he has been through, the boy is still just a 'toy' for the Foundation's medical trainees.

item.title: Small language issues and suggested corrections
item.content:encoded:
Here's a collapsible with most of the small language issues I could find:

+ show block
– hide block

During treatments, one security staff is to be presented outside the perimeter.

"Presented" is odd there. I'd go with: "During treatments, one member of security staff must be present outside the perimeter."

Such arrangement is made not due to any threat from SCP-1830-B's part, but solely to reduce surprise caused by its sudden appearance.

"This security arrangement exists not due to any threat from SCP-1830-B, but solely to reduce surprise caused by its sudden appearance."

Any request to utilize SCP-1830-A for comatose-related training should be made in Form-1830-MT and deliver to Sector-07's site director.

I'm not sure what "comatose-related training" is exactly? And "deliver" should be "delivered".

At the time of containment, the subject is measured at a height of 1.6 meters and weighs 51 kg.

I'd go with: "At the time of containment, the subject is 1.6 meters in height and weighs 51 kg."

Incised wounds, curved into derogatory phrases in traditional Chinese characters, are found on the pelvis and inner thighs.

Should be "carved into derogatory phrases", I think?

Some interviewees attribute it to Asperger syndrome, with which SCP-1830-A was diagnosed.

"Some interviewees attribute this to SCP-1830-A's diagnosis of Asperger syndrome."

It is unclear whether these were coping mechanisms against difficult circumstances in school, neglect on the part of the subject's sole parent, or is related to SCP-1830-B.

"It is unclear whether these behaviors were coping mechanisms against difficult circumstances in school, neglect on the part of the subject's sole parent, or related to SCP-1830-B."

The German Army in World War One (3): 1917-1918, Osprey Publishing. Extensive handwritten translations were found in "Weapon", "Training" and illustration plates' commentary sections.

As it'd be a section heading in the book, it probably should be "Weapons" rather than "Weapon".

Defence International No. 340, Defence International Inc. A Taiwanese periodical on current military affair. Fifty-two magazines belong to this series are found in SCP-1830-A's room.

"A Taiwanese periodical on current military affairs. Fifty-two magazines belonging to this series are found in SCP-1830-A's room."

School diary The photograph on the "Personal Information" page was torn. The rest of the page was overwritten in derogatory words not consistent to SCP-1830-A's handwriting.

"not consistent with SCP-1830-A's handwriting."

Swiss Army knife DNA testing of blood strain on the blade matched SCP-1830-A.

"blood stain"

Note: except for specified, most books are found with extensive drawings of military vehicles, firearms, and figures in uniforms.

"except as specified, most books were found with extensive drawings"

When movement is made, SCP-1830-B's visibility becomes inversely proportionally related to the distance with SCP-1830-A, with complete disappearance occurring at five meters.

I'd go with: "When SCP-1830-B moves, its visibility is inversely proportional to its distance from SCP-1830-A, with complete disappearance occurring at five meters."

violent, desperate gesture on the part of SCP-1830-B.

"desperate gestures"

SCP-1830-B manifests alternatively in two humanoid forms:

"SCP-1830-B can manifest in two distinct humanoid forms:"

"Ludwig", a middle-age Caucasian male bearing appearance of WW1-era German soldier in the Sturmtruppen formations.

"middle-aged Caucasian male with the appearance of a WW1-era German soldier in the Sturmtruppen formations."

an Asian male in late twenties of age, bearing appearance of Chinese soldier in the National Revolutionary Army circa 1930s.

"an Asian male who appears to be in his late twenties, with the appearance of a Chinese soldier in the National Revolutionary Army circa 1930s."

SCP-1830 was brought to Foundation's attention

Either "Foundation attention" or "the Foundation's attention".

N: Who know? Slaving on his computer like every Post-90s I guess? I heard chatters from his room. Perhaps he did chat with friends online, afterall.

"N: Who knows? Slaving on his computer like every Post-90s I guess? I heard chatter from his room. Perhaps he did chat with friends online, after all."

(Note: subsequent inspection found no peripheral capable of verbal chat on SCP-1830-A’s custom-built desktop.

This confused me initially. I think "vocal chat" would work better, if the sense meant is that there was no microphone or equivalent peripheral.

Sometime he muttered something like that’s how you use a gun, and a bunch of other probably military terms - he likes those stuffs you know, his room’s lined with books about jets and guns.

"Sometimes he muttered something like that’s how you use a gun, and a bunch of other probably military terms - he likes that stuff you know, his room’s lined with books about jets and guns."

Someone squat down and fingered his cheeks, then this bully with buckteeth took his eyeglasses. He tried to take it back, but they just pressed him on the ground.

"Someone squatted down and fingered his cheeks, then this bully with buckteeth took his eyeglasses. He tried to take them back, but they just pressed him on the ground."

It must be pretty rough because that boy was jerking and moaning. He didn't even crying for help, just turned his head away and let the tears fell.

"It must have been pretty rough because that boy was jerking and moaning. He didn't even cry for help, just turned his head away and let the tears fall."

Yea I am going into that.

"Yeah I am going into that." English idiom would make "I am getting to that." more usual, but that could just be a translation thing.

he stopped resisting even when those bullies kept wrecking his bottom.

"wrecking his bottom" is, probably inadvertently, a slightly amusing way to phrase it. I'm not sure what a better rephrase would be, though.

I saw the flash but it's completely silent.

"I saw the flash but it was completely silent."

I expected someone to fell, instead those guys were still jerking the boy as if nothing has happened.

"I expected someone to fall, instead those guys were still jerking the boy as if nothing had happened."

That sick-fuck just kept carving to pictures on the boy's [REDACTED] as if nothing happened.

"That sick-fuck just kept carving pictures on the boy's [REDACTED] as if nothing happened. "

Footnotes

4. Cross-references between that name and facial appearance with personnel archival of the Imperial German Army have yet to produce result.

"Cross-references between this name and facial appearance with personnel archives of the Imperial German Army have yet to produce result."

5. A Hong Kong slang, equivalent to “Generation Y” in the West

"5. A term used in Hong Kong slang, equivalent to “Generation Y” in the West."

Congratulations again on a very solid SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is a great article. Well done in so, so many ways.

+1

(And for the record, my immediate Audience's Reaction was feeling very frustrated over the lack of retribution on the bullies. Which's a good sign, I guess)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is good. There's a large amount of small issues with the language I'd love to help fix, but the core of it is well-executed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Poor kid. This is a really well done article though. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Trust me, there's more than enough parents who will passively watch their kids get traumatized and develop very serious mental problems. All the while standing by saying: "I did my best."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I would really like to like this, but the interviews just killed it for me. I don't think the mother or the eyewitness talk like real people do. Even the worst mother in the world would become attentive when her son yells

‘butcher them’, ‘burn alive’, ‘die die die’

No vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Stroking SCP-1830-A's head and whispering into his ear.

Oh god my heart
Take this +1, you magnificent bastard.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is… really, really good. I'm impressed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very well done, Andy. You have my enthusiastic upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh, okay. Feel free to change that bit back.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: He can project humanoid-like manifestation from his mind, but it's strictly a projection. It can't really affect reality or even corporeal objects. So I think he's a long way from the definition of reality bender

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Could…could this mean he's an extremely low level reality bender? +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This needs the odd bit of proofreading, but otherwise I like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks very much! but there's a tiny bit that deviate from my original meaning:

Agent ████, an undercover agent embedded in the Hong Kong Police Force (HKPF), noticed anomalous sightings at the crime scene, in SCP-1830-A's emergency ward, and in witness' testimony

I mean to say that he learnt of those anomalous sightings in witness' testimony

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ohhhhh. Thank you for that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Because he did not. In fact, it's the most interesting part about SCP-1830-B. To quote my reply on the draft forum:

"My idea for B is that it’s a manifestation of A’s desires: Ling-kuo stems from his desire to be protected and love; while Ludwig is an avatar of his anger and desire to revenge. In other word, they are “defensive” and “offensive” mentality. Due to the bullying and neglect, A retreated to his favorite hobby – reading on military and history. There he found two entities that he empathizes with: the National Revolutionary Army, a poorly equipped force that hopelessly resisted the Japanese invasion (his situation); and the Sturmtruppen, an offensive formation that specialized in brutal close quarter fighting in the trench (what he wants to happen on the bullies & his daily experience). B thus come into being from the details found on books."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this one. The back story pulls some nice emotional strings and the concept is fairly interesting. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really like this article. I'm a huge military history buff, and I have a particular interest in WWI and the Chinese Civil War. And it's well written and provokes feelings in my stony heart to boot. This is the sort of article I would write if I was a much better writer.

That being said, I have one question: How can a present-day high school student have a father who fought in the KMT in the 1930s? I mean, it's possible for an 80-year old man to father children, but not really likely.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There are a lot of grammatical oddities and errors here. I'll get around to fixing them once I'm done reading.

Having finished, I don't 100% know what's going on here but I definitely like it.

E: I had to read it a second time during the editing process and ;~;

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: fixed. Thanks again

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Needs an object. "Make what appear to be whispering [noun]".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks:). but what in that sentence need to be fixed?
My grammar is pretty bad.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Whoa. This is fantastic.

stroke SCP-1830-A's head and make what appear to be whispering

This needs fixed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
WHY DO I GET SO MANY FEELS

Seriously, this is absolutely incredible. By far one of the best skips on the site. I love everything about it, the story, the concept, and I nearly cried at the sketch.

I don't even think I need the tell you what my vote was.

item.title: Special thanks
item.content:encoded:
Special thanks to Dr Clockwork, SoullessSingularity, Azzleflux, SpoonOfEvil, Waterfire and my friend Dylan for your feedbacks!
And Djoric, Scantron for your grammatical fix!

DISCLAIMER: The drawing is edited from one of my artwork posted in DeviantART and the Wrong Planet forum. It is NOT a pirated work because I'm the artist.
